title: Fix SwarmDetailView to auto-inject current swarm ID when creating law tokens

requirements.text: In SwarmDetailView, when the Yes or No plus button is clicked to open the CreateLawTokenDialog, automatically pass the currently displayed swarm's ID as the parent swarm ID into the dialog's creation call. The swarm ID must be read directly from the swarm detail view's own state/props — no user input or confirmation required. The dialog must call the backend's law token creation function with this swarm ID correctly populated so that the 'Parent Swarm does not exist' error no longer occurs.

quotes: the relevant swarm ID should be automatically fed, without user confirmation
The plus buttons in SwarmDetailView are not passing the current swarm's ID correctly when calling the create law token function
I'll make it a focused single-file change to the SwarmDetailView component to ensure the current swarm's ID is automatically injected into the law token creation call

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Yes or No plus button on a swarm's detail view opens the CreateLawTokenDialog without triggering 'Parent Swarm does not exist' error
The current swarm's ID is automatically injected into the law token creation call without any user input
A law token is successfully created and associated with the correct parent swarm under the Yes or No location
After successful creation, the UI remains on the swarm detail view page
The new law token appears in the correct Yes or No panel of the swarm detail view after creation

constraints: Only modify SwarmDetailView and/or CreateLawTokenDialog — do not change backend logic or other frontend files unless strictly necessary
Do not require user confirmation or additional UI steps to pass the swarm ID
UI must stay on the swarm detail view page after law token creation
nonGoals: Changing the backend createLocation or law token creation logic
Modifying any other view or dialog unrelated to law token creation from SwarmDetailView
Adding new navigation flows after law token creation
